\text{Step 1: Dive in with the Law of Sines to find the first angle } B_1.

\frac{\sin(B)}{b} = \frac{\sin(A)}{a}

\sin(B) = \frac{11.3\sin(39^\circ)}{7.9} \approx 0.9002

B_1 = \sin^{-1}(0.9002) \approx 64.2^\circ

\text{Step 2: Check if a second triangle is possible.}

\text{The theoretical second angle is } B_2 = 180^\circ - 64.2^\circ = 115.8^\circ.

\text{Check if it fits with the given angle } A: 39^\circ + 115.8^\circ = 154.8^\circ.

\text{Since } 154.8^\circ < 180^\circ \text{, there is room for a third angle! There are exactly TWO valid triangles.}

---

\text{Step 3: Solve Triangle 1 (using } B_1 \text{).}

\angle C_1 = 180^\circ - \angle A - B_1 \approx 180^\circ - 39^\circ - 64.2^\circ = 76.8^\circ

c_1 = \frac{a\sin(C_1)}{\sin(A)}

c_1 = \frac{7.9\sin(76.8^\circ)}{\sin(39^\circ)} \approx 12.22

---

\text{Step 4: Solve Triangle 2 (using } B_2 \text{).}

\angle C_2 = 180^\circ - \angle A - B_2 \approx 180^\circ - 39^\circ - 115.8^\circ = 25.2^\circ

c_2 = \frac{a\sin(C_2)}{\sin(A)}

c_2 = \frac{7.9\sin(25.2^\circ)}{\sin(39^\circ)} \approx 5.34
